mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in the effective federal funds rate signals a monetary tightening cycle. This increases the discount rate for corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative forward returns for equities as valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has been hiking rates since mid-2004
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 6-month change in FEDFUNDS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, sustained increase in the unemployment rate is a primary indicator of recession. This signal precedes declines in corporate earnings and consumer demand, causing negative equity returns as the market prices in a downturn.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low.
evidence_refs: fact:Sahm's Rule as a recession indicator concept
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event during a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When strong nominal retail sales growth occurs simultaneously with accelerating inflation, it signals overheating demand that will provoke an aggressive, hawkish monetary policy response from the Federal Reserve, negatively impacting equity markets.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in RSAFS > 5% and year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:Fed's dual mandate includes price stability
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return is non-negative during periods when the trigger condition is met over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects a robust real economy and supports corporate revenue growth. This fundamental strength translates into positive equity returns, especially over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 12-month change in INDPRO and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a robust labor market, which translates to higher aggregate income, consumer confidence, and corporate revenues, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:NFP is a major market-moving economic release
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return following a top-quintile monthly PAYEMS print is lower than the average return following a bottom-quintile print.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts, a key interest-rate sensitive sector, signals that monetary tightening is effectively slowing the economy. This is a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown, negatively affecting equities.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 20% from its 12-month peak.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector is a leading indicator of economic cycles
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in the 12 months following the satisfaction of the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve is raising rates while inflation is also accelerating, the resulting increase in real interest rates provides a strong headwind for risk assets by increasing the attractiveness of risk-free assets and tightening financial conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:Real interest rates are a key driver of asset valuation models
failure_condition: A strategy going long equities when the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are both positive does not underperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a full cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly print in retail sales provides a positive sentiment shock regarding the health of the US consumer, who drives a majority of economic activity. This leads to positive equity performance over a very short-term horizon.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following the release of the retail sales report is not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.10) on days with top-quartile growth.
